1 Initial Data Analysis

Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggpubr)
library(janitor)
library(hms)
library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
theme_set(theme_bw())
x = readxl::read_excel("housing-prices-ge19.xls")
Code 
variable_table <- x %>%
  # Get the names of the columns (variables)
  names() %>%
  # Convert the vector of names into a tibble
  tibble::enframe(name = NULL, value = "Variable_Name") %>%
  # Add a second column for the data type (class) of each variable
  dplyr::mutate(
    Data_Type = purrr::map_chr(x, class)
  )
variable_table
# A tibble: 17 × 2
   Variable_Name Data_Type
   <chr>         <chr>    
 1 Price         numeric  
 2 Lot.Size      numeric  
 3 Waterfront    numeric  
 4 Age           numeric  
 5 Land.Value    numeric  
 6 New.Construct numeric  
 7 Central.Air   numeric  
 8 Fuel.Type     character
 9 Heat.Type     character
10 Sewer.Type    character
11 Living.Area   numeric  
12 Pct.College   numeric  
13 Bedrooms      numeric  
14 Fireplaces    numeric  
15 Bathrooms     numeric  
16 Rooms         numeric  
17 Test          numeric

2 Linearity

2.1 Continuous Numeric Variables - Scatter Plots

2.1.1 Age, Land Value, Living Area, Lot Size

Code 
numeric_vars <- x %>% 
  select(Price, Lot.Size, Age, Land.Value, Living.Area)

plot_data_numeric <- numeric_vars %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Price,
    names_to = "Predictor",
    values_to = "Value"
  )

ggplot(plot_data_numeric, aes(x = Value, y = Price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Plot the individual data points
  # Add a linear model trend line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "red") + 
  facet_wrap(~Predictor, scales = "free_x") + # Create a plot for each predictor
  labs(title = "Price vs Continuous Numeric Variables",
       y = "Price") +
  theme_minimal()

2.2 Categorical Variables - Box Plots

2.2.1 Fireplaces

Code 
x$Fireplaces <- as.factor(x$Fireplaces)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Fireplaces)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Number of Fireplaces",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Number of Fireplaces")

2.2.2 Waterfront

Code 
x$Waterfront <- as.factor(x$Waterfront)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Waterfront)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Waterfront Status",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Waterfront")

2.2.3 New Construct

Code 
x$New.Construct <- as.factor(x$New.Construct)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = New.Construct)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by New Construct Status",
       x = "Price",
       y = "New Construct")

       x = "Price",
       y = "Fuel Type")

2.2.8 Bathrooms

Code

ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Bathrooms)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Bathrooms",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Bathrooms")

2.2.9 Bedrooms

Code

ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Bedrooms)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Bedrooms",
       x = "Price",

2.2.10 Central Air

Code

ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Central.Air)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Central Air Status",
       x = "Price",

## Initial Data Analysis

```{r, results='hide'}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggpubr)
library(janitor)
library(hms)
library(gt)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
theme_set(theme_bw())
x = readxl::read_excel("housing-prices-ge19.xls")
```

```{r}
variable_table <- x %>%
  # Get the names of the columns (variables)
  names() %>%
  # Convert the vector of names into a tibble
  tibble::enframe(name = NULL, value = "Variable_Name") %>%
  # Add a second column for the data type (class) of each variable
  dplyr::mutate(
    Data_Type = purrr::map_chr(x, class)
  )
variable_table
```

## Linearity 

### Continuous Numeric Variables - Scatter Plots

#### Age, Land Value, Living Area, Lot Size

```{r}
numeric_vars <- x %>% 
  select(Price, Lot.Size, Age, Land.Value, Living.Area)

plot_data_numeric <- numeric_vars %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Price,
    names_to = "Predictor",
    values_to = "Value"
  )

ggplot(plot_data_numeric, aes(x = Value, y = Price)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Plot the individual data points
  # Add a linear model trend line
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "red") + 
  facet_wrap(~Predictor, scales = "free_x") + # Create a plot for each predictor
  labs(title = "Price vs Continuous Numeric Variables",
       y = "Price") +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Categorical Variables - Box Plots

#### Fireplaces 

```{r}
x$Fireplaces <- as.factor(x$Fireplaces)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Fireplaces)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Number of Fireplaces",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Number of Fireplaces")
```

#### Waterfront 

```{r}
x$Waterfront <- as.factor(x$Waterfront)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Waterfront)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Waterfront Status",
       x = "Price",
       y = "Waterfront")
```

#### New Construct 

```{r}
x$New.Construct <- as.factor(x$New.Construct)
ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = New.Construct)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by New Construct Status",
       x = "Price",
       y = "New Construct")
```

```{r}

ggplot(x, aes(x = Price, y = Central.Air)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Central Air Status",
```
